member.name: F:EasyData.AspNetCore.EasyDataApiHandler.Manager
member.summary: An instance of EasyDatasManager that incapsulates all business logic of EasyData API

member.name: F:EasyData.AspNetCore.EasyDataApiHandler.Options
member.summary: Some options that affects handler's behavior

member.name: M:EasyData.AspNetCore.EasyDataApiHandler.#ctor(EasyData.Services.EasyDataManager,EasyData.Services.EasyDataOptions,Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The instance of EasyDataManager that incapsulates all business logic of EasyData API.
Some options that affects handler's behavior.

member.name: M:EasyData.AspNetCore.EasyDataApiHandler.HandleGetModelAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles the "GetModel" request as an asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:EasyData.AspNetCore.EasyDataApiHandler.WriteGetModelResponseAsync(Newtonsoft.Json.JsonWriter,EasyData.MetaData,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Writes the model's content to the JsonWriter. Can be overridden in the derived classes to write some extra data to the response for GetModel request .

member.name: M:EasyData.AspNetCore.EasyDataApiHandler.HandleExceptionAsync(System.Exception,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles exceptions that might occur inside any other EasyData API request handler. If the writing to the response content has not started it returns a response with 400 code and the error message.

member.name: M:EasyData.AspNetCore.EasyDataApiHandler.WriteJsonResponseAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext,System.String,System.Func{Newtonsoft.Json.JsonWriter,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.Task},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Writes a response in JSON format. This function creates a HttpResponseStreamWriter and invokes the callback function which actually does the job.
member.param: The value of result property in the response object (either "ok" or any other string).
The function that actually writes the content.

member.name: M:EasyData.AspNetCore.EasyDataApiHandler.WriteOkJsonResponseAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext,System.Func{Newtonsoft.Json.JsonWriter,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.Task},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Writes an OK response in JSON format. This function actually calls with "ok" value for the "result" parameter.
member.param: The function that actually writes the content.

member.name: M:EasyData.AspNetCore.EasyDataApiHandler.WriteErrorJsonResponseAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Writes an ERROR response in JSON format. This function actually calls with "error" value for the "result" parameter. It also writes a "message" property to the response object with the value passed in "errorMessage" parameter.
